license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.eahbab6
title: Electron microscopy for virus identification and virus assemblage characterization
author: Kenneth M. Stedman

steps_raw.instruction: 5µL of an enrichment culture, or 0.2 µm filtered and centrifuged (10 min at 3000g) cell-free supernatant, is spotted onto carbon/formvar-coated electron microscope grids (Ted Pella or EM Sciences). 00:10:00
It is then allowed to absorb for 2 min. 00:02:00

Remove sample from grid by slowly bringing a small (ca. 1cm 2 ) piece of filter paper perpendicular to the grid to the side of the grid. The sample will be removed by wicking. Stain grid by placing on a 5μL drop of 2% Uranyl Acetate (or Phosphotungstate) for 15-30 seconds. Remove stain by wicking as above. Air dry for at least 10 minutes VLPs can generally be discerned at ×16,000–20,000 magnification (Fig. 2 in guidelines).
Samples are examined by transmission electron microscopy (TEM), e.g., JEOL 100 cx, operated at 100 keV. VLPs can generally be discerned at ×16,000–20,000 magnification (Fig. 2 in guidelines).
